title: Basic enum
instruction:
Define an enum and use its values.

Run this.
successMessage: Enum values are auto-numbered starting at 0. Casting to int gives the underlying value.

title: Enum in switch
instruction:
Switch on enums for exhaustive case handling.

Run this.
successMessage: Switch expressions on enums are exhaustive — all cases handled. The compiler warns if you miss a case.

title: Custom underlying values
instruction:
You can assign explicit integer values to enum members.

Run this.
successMessage: Custom values make enums map to real-world codes. Casting an int back to an enum gives you the named value.

title: Flags enum
instruction:
`[Flags]` enums use powers of 2 to combine values with bitwise OR.

Run this.
successMessage: Flags enums combine permissions with |. HasFlag checks if a permission is included. Admin = all three combined.

title: Enum.GetValues
instruction:
Iterate all values of an enum with `Enum.GetValues`.

Run this.
successMessage: Enum.GetValues<T>() returns all enum values. Useful for populating dropdowns or iterating over all options.

title: Enum.Parse and TryParse
instruction:
Convert strings to enum values at runtime.

Run this.
successMessage: Enum.Parse throws on invalid values. TryParse returns false safely. Use TryParse for user input.

title: Enum with methods via extension
instruction:
Enums cannot have methods, but extension methods add behaviour.

Run this.
successMessage: Extension methods let you add behaviour to enums without modifying them. this DayOfWeek2 day makes it callable as d.IsWeekend().

title: Enum as array index
instruction:
Enum values as array indices create type-safe lookup tables.

Run this.
successMessage: Casting to int uses enum values as array indices. A clean way to associate data with each enum value.
